message:
fix(schema): remove DESC from active list index to eliminate false positive

Frankensqlite's B-tree stores DESC index entries in a different physical
order than C sqlite3 expects, causing PRAGMA integrity_check to report
"entries are out of order for their declared key directions" — even on
freshly created databases.

Fix: change idx_issues_list_active_order from (priority, created_at DESC)
to (priority, created_at).  SQLite's query planner can efficiently
reverse-scan an ASC index for ORDER BY ... created_at DESC queries;
the performance difference is negligible for typical issue counts.

Adds schema version 5 migration that drops the old index so the
idempotent CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS recreates it with ASC ordering.
Existing databases are migrated on first open.

Part of #245.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
